predictions.text: CISA will add CVE-2026-3888 (Snap local privilege-escalation) to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog.
predictions.verification_criteria: CVE-2026-3888 appears as a new entry in CISA’s official KEV Catalog (current catalog or release notes) on or before the deadline.
evidence: Qualys write-up describing Snap LPE to root (CVE-2026-3888): https://blog.qualys.com/vulnerabilities-threat-research/2026/03/17/cve-2026-3888-important-snap-flaw-enables-local-privilege-escalation-to-root
monitoring_signals: CISA KEV Catalog updates (diffs/feeds)
Vendor advisories for snapd/Snap packages
Public exploit/IOC reports linking CVE-2026-3888 to in-the-wild activity
predictions.rationale: High-impact LPEs with broad Linux footprint often get KEV if exploitation is reported; disclosure timing suggests a reasonable chance of downstream exploitation signals.

predictions.text: Qatar LNG exports will experience a disruption: Ras Laffan-related LNG loadings/exports will be at least 20% below the February 2026 weekly average for at least 2 consecutive weeks.
predictions.verification_criteria: Kpler or Vortexa (or an IEA report citing such tracking) shows Qatar LNG exports/loadings for two consecutive calendar weeks averaging >=20% below the February 2026 weekly average, with reporting clearly tying the disruption to Ras Laffan/Qatar facility impacts or security constraints.
evidence: Report of damage to Qatar facility tied to major LNG complex amid Iran conflict: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/iran-war-qatar-ras-laffan-natural-gas-lng.html
Hormuz bottlenecks and shipping risk premia discussion: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/hormuz-bottleneck-vessel-tanker-tracker-shipping-strait-of-hormuz.html
monitoring_signals: Kpler/Vortexa weekly Qatar LNG export estimates
Ras Laffan operational statements from QatarEnergy
LNG vessel AIS rerouting/queueing near Hormuz
JKM/TTF prompt price spikes tied to supply outage headlines
predictions.rationale: Physical damage plus heightened shipping/security risk raises near-term outage probability, though Qatar typically has redundancy and strong incentives to maintain flows.

predictions.text: ICE Endex Dutch TTF natural gas front-month will settle at or above €80/MWh on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: ICE Endex (or widely cited market data providers reporting ICE Endex settlements) shows the TTF front-month futures contract settlement price >= 80.00 EUR/MWh on any trading day by the deadline.
evidence: Rising Strait of Hormuz bottleneck risk affecting global LNG flows: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/hormuz-bottleneck-vessel-tanker-tracker-shipping-strait-of-hormuz.html
monitoring_signals: TTF forward curve and implied volatility
EU LNG send-out and storage trajectory
News on Qatari LNG output, maintenance, or security incidents
JKM-TTF spread widening
predictions.rationale: European gas is highly sensitive to marginal LNG disruptions; €80 is a plausible stress print if outage/rerouting risk persists, but not a base case.

predictions.text: The U.S. will extend or re-issue a Jones Act waiver for petroleum product shipments for at least an additional 30 days beyond the initial 60-day period announced in March 2026.
predictions.verification_criteria: A formal U.S. government notice (DHS/MARAD/DOE or White House release) announces an extension/renewal of the March 2026 Jones Act waiver (or a substantively similar new waiver) for petroleum/crude movements, with the extension period being >=30 days.
evidence: U.S. issued a 60-day Jones Act waiver to ease domestic fuel logistics: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/trump-jones-act-oil-iran-war.html
monitoring_signals: DHS/MARAD waiver postings and press releases
EIA PADD-to-PADD product flow tightness and regional price spreads
Political statements on fuel price stabilization
Refinery outages and inventory draws in key regions
predictions.rationale: If conflict-driven logistics and regional price dislocations persist, policymakers may roll the waiver to avoid renewed bottlenecks; if prices normalize quickly, extension is less likely.

predictions.text: BLS will report Final Demand Producer Price Index (PPI) year-over-year inflation at or above 4.5% in at least one monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: In any BLS monthly PPI news release through the deadline, the ‘Final Demand’ PPI (unadjusted 12-month percent change) is reported as >= 4.5%.
evidence: February PPI rose 0.7% (services +0.5%, goods +1.1%), indicating upstream pressure: https://www.bls.gov/news.release/archives/ppi_03182026.htm
monitoring_signals: Monthly BLS PPI releases (final demand headline and details)
Energy and transportation input cost indicators
ISM prices paid components
Corporate commentary on pass-through pricing
predictions.rationale: A large monthly print plus energy shock risk increases odds of an elevated YoY reading, but base effects and normalization could keep YoY below 4.5%.

predictions.text: BLS CPI will show Core CPI (CPI less food and energy) month-over-month at or above 0.40% in at least one monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Any BLS CPI news release through the deadline reports seasonally adjusted Core CPI (CPI-U less food and energy) m/m change >= 0.40%.
evidence: Fed emphasized inflation focus and uncertainty from the Iran war/energy shock: https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260318a.htm
Upstream inflation pressure indicated by elevated PPI: https://www.bls.gov/news.release/archives/ppi_03182026.htm
monitoring_signals: Monthly CPI releases (core m/m and key categories)
High-frequency rent and services inflation indicators
Energy-related pass-through into core services (transportation, insurance)
Fed communications highlighting inflation persistence
predictions.rationale: With services inflation sticky and upstream pressures elevated, a 0.40% core print is plausible at least once, though shelter cooling could offset.

predictions.text: Micron will raise its FY2026 revenue guidance by at least $2.0 billion versus the immediately prior official company guidance range/point.
predictions.verification_criteria: In an earnings release, investor presentation, or 10-Q/10-K, Micron states updated FY2026 revenue guidance that is >= $2.0B higher than the most recent previously issued FY2026 revenue guidance (comparing midpoints if ranges are provided).
evidence: Micron revenue nearly tripled and beat estimates on AI-driven memory demand: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/micron-mu-q2-earnings-report-2026.html
monitoring_signals: Micron earnings releases and call transcripts
DRAM/NAND contract and spot pricing trends
Hyperscaler AI capex revisions and memory content per server trends
Micron commentary on supply tightness and ASPs
predictions.rationale: Strong AI-linked demand can drive rapid upward revisions, but memory cycles remain volatile and supply responses could cap guidance upgrades.

predictions.text: DoD will publicly award (announce) a contract or OTA agreement worth at least $250 million for frontier AI model access/services where the prime recipient is Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, or Meta (i.e., not OpenAI).
predictions.verification_criteria: A DoD/Defense.gov contract announcement, press release, or official procurement disclosure (or a reputable outlet citing official award documents) confirms an award >= $250M for AI model access/inference/training/services to one of the named primes, excluding OpenAI as the prime.
evidence: DoD reportedly flagged Anthropic constraints as national-security risk amid competition: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/18/dod-says-anthropics-red-lines-make-it-an-unacceptable-risk-to-national-security/
monitoring_signals: Defense.gov daily contract announcements
SAM.gov/FPDS award entries for AI model services
DoD CDAO procurement updates and budget documents
Vendor public-sector partnership announcements
predictions.rationale: Competitive dynamics and procurement scaling make a large award possible, but $250M+ and public disclosure timing introduce meaningful uncertainty.

predictions.text: Google Chrome will ship a stable release enabling a post-quantum/hybrid key exchange for TLS by default for at least 50% of Chrome stable users (as stated in a Chrome release note, Chromium blog, or Google security blog).
predictions.verification_criteria: Official Google/Chromium release communications explicitly state that a PQC/hybrid TLS key exchange is enabled by default and rolled out to >=50% of stable-channel users (or ‘default for stable’ with an accompanying statement implying majority rollout) by the deadline.
evidence: Strategic momentum in quantum cryptography ecosystem highlighted by Turing Award focus: https://awards.acm.org/about/2025-turing
monitoring_signals: Chrome/Chromium release notes and security blog posts
IETF TLS PQC/hybrid standardization milestones
Telemetry or field reports on negotiated key shares
Major CDN announcements (Cloudflare/Akamai/Fastly) supporting PQC/hybrid at scale
predictions.rationale: PQC deployment has been progressing in stages; majority-by-default is plausible within months but depends on standardization, interoperability, and performance considerations.
